macro_segments.title: Neshech and Tarbit: Distinguishing Terms and Their Application to Money and Food

micro_segments.title: Initial Beraita: Neshech applies to money, Tarbit to food in Vayikra; Devarim applies Neshech to both
Ein li ela beloveh: The prohibition applies specifically to the borrower; how we learn it applies to the lender via Gezeirah Shavah
Minayin lerabot kol davar: Extension to all objects beyond food and money via Devarim
Ravina's alternative reading: Juxtaposition of Neshech and Marbit in Vayikra eliminates need for Gezeirah Shavah
Gemara's response: Gezeirah Shavah still needed for application to all objects (kol davar asher yishach)

macro_segments.title: Rava's Analysis: Why separate prohibitions of Ribis, Ona'ah, and Gezel are necessary

micro_segments.title: Rava's opening question: Why does Torah specify prohibitions of Ribis, Ona'ah, and Gezel separately?
Why Ribis cannot be inferred from Gezel: Ribis is prohibited even to the borrower, unlike theft
Why Ona'ah cannot be inferred from Gezel: Ona'ah involves willing consent, unlike theft against will
Analysis of potential common denominator arguments and why each pairing fails
The actual common denominator: All three prohibit ill-gotten gain (Hatzad Hashaveh: Gozelo)
Why Gezel still requires explicit prohibition: To teach Kovesh Sechar Sachir (withholding worker's wages)
Tanu Rabanan: False measures, liquid measures, and land measurement prohibitions explained

macro_segments.title: Yetzias Mitzrayim: Divine Discernment and Vigilance in Hidden Fraud

micro_segments.title: Rava's interpretation: God's ability to distinguish firstborn in Egypt parallels vigilance over hidden fraud in business
Cases of hidden fraud: Ribis via non-Jew agent, weights in salt, false techeiles in tzitzis
Rav Chanina's question: Why invoke Yetzias Mitzrayim regarding non-kosher fish innards?
The word Hama'aleh: Elevation and refinement of character through observance of kashrut
Amir's response: Even without greater reward, elevated status (mea'isa) refines the person

macro_segments.title: Ribis Ketsutzah vs. Avak Ribis: Restitution obligations and inheritance rules

micro_segments.title: Amar Raba: Distinction between Torah-level explicit Ribis (Ketsutzah) and Rabbinic Ribis (Avak)
Ad kan yachin rasha veilbash tzadik: Father collects Ribis but son inherits it without obligation to return
Rabbi Eliezer: Ribis Ketsutzah leaves via courts; Avak Ribis does not
Rabbi Yochanan: Even Ribis Ketsutzah does not leave via courts because it is not Gezel
Rabbi Yitzchak's support: Pasuk in Yechezkel compares to murderers—restitution impossible, only divine consequence
Rava's interpretation: Ribis not restorable like murder; oppression dynamic cannot be rectified
Rabbi Eliezer's source: Vechi achicha imach—obligation to return to restore brotherhood
Rabbi Yochanan's reconciliation: Uses Vechi achicha imach for different teaching on priority of life (Ben Petura vs. Rabbi Akiva)

topical_tags.term: Avak Ribis (Dust of Ribis, Rabbinic Ribis)
Damav Bo (Blood on his head—capital crime language)
Vechi Achicha Imach (And your brother shall live with you)
Chayecha Kodmim Lechayei Achicha (Your life takes precedence over your friend's)
